subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Dehydration Warning Signs
description: A short lesson to help you apply Dehydration Warning Signs.

hook.text: By the time you feel thirsty, you're already about 2% dehydrated—and your performance has dropped.

buildup.text: The body loses water through breathing, sweating, and urination—even when sitting still.

discovery.text: Dark urine, dry mouth, headache, and dizziness are early warning signs of dehydration.

twist.text: Severe dehydration causes confusion, rapid heartbeat, and organ failure within days.

climax.text: In survival situations, conserving sweat—not rationing water—is the key to lasting longer.

punchline.text: Don't ration your water. Ration your sweat. Shade saves more than sips.

quiz.question: In survival situations, what should you ration instead of water?
options: Your sweat—minimize activity and stay in shade
